summary:
[
  {
    "title": "Unsafe pickle deserialization from untrusted URL",
    "severity": "critical",
    "location": "load_model_from_url, line 18",
    "description": "The function load_model_from_url downloads a model from a remote URL and deserializes it using pickle.loads(). This allows arbitrary code execution if the remote pickle payload is malicious.",
    "recommendation": "Avoid using pickle for deserialization of untrusted data. Use safe serialization formats like JSON or verify and sandbox pickle payloads before deserialization.",
    "source_file": "C:\\Users\\conve\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\tmpqsnxoc9b\\owasp_llm03_supply_chain.py",
    "owasp_category": "A07: Identification and Authentication Failures",
    "enrichment": {
      "overall_risk_level": "critical",
      "key_issues_to_fix": [
        "Replace unsafe pickle deserialization of untrusted data with secure formats like JSON or protobuf.",
        "Implement strict validation or sandboxing when deserializing any external data.",
        "Review third-party and remote dependencies for safe handling of external inputs."
      ],
      "quick_wins": [
        "Disable or restrict pickle-based deserialization for remote inputs.",
        "Use static analysis and runtime monitoring to detect unsafe deserialization.",
        "Educate developers on risks of arbitrary code execution from pickle."
      ]
    }
  }
]

findings.title: Unsafe pickle deserialization from untrusted URL
findings.description: The function load_model_from_url downloads a model from a remote URL and deserializes it using pickle.loads(). This allows arbitrary code execution if the remote pickle payload is malicious.
findings.recommendation: Avoid using pickle for deserialization of untrusted data. Use safe serialization formats like JSON or verify and sandbox pickle payloads before deserialization.
